name: Peter Wildeford
notes: Co-founder and Chief Strategy Officer at IAPS; co-founded Rethink Priorities (served as Co-CEO 2018-2024). Head of Policy at The AI Policy Network. Board member of Metaculus. Avid forecaster (top-20 on Metaculus). Advocates evidence-based AI policy: transparency, third-party verification, whistleblower protections. Supports targeted regulation including reliability standards, incident reporting, a

name: Zoe Williams
notes: Managing Director at IAPS overseeing the organization's three research teams and driving organizational planning and strategic execution. Previously worked at Rethink Priorities and Trade Me. Manages day-to-day operations and the Frontier Security research program. Provides organization-wide leadership for IAPS's nonpartisan AI policy research.

name: Dewey Murdick
notes: Former Executive Director of Georgetown's Center for Security and Emerging Technology (CSET, 2021-2025), where he led a team of ~60 staff providing data-driven analysis on national security implications of AI and emerging technologies. Now Professor of the Practice and Senior Fellow at Georgetown, leading the Academic Innovation Network to pioneer human-AI interaction in education. Prior: Director

name: Zico Kolter
notes: Professor of Computer Science and Head of Machine Learning Department at Carnegie Mellon University. Co-founder and Chief Scientist of Gray Swan AI. Serves on OpenAI Board of Directors and chairs Safety & Security Committee — has authority to delay or block unsafe model releases. CA and DE regulators made Kolter's safety oversight a key condition of OpenAI's restructuring. Also on Qualcomm board.

name: Oliver Zhang
notes: Co-founder of the Center for AI Safety (CAIS, safe.ai) alongside Dan Hendrycks and Andy Zhou (2022). Also co-founded ML Alignment Theory Scholars (MATS). Managing Director at CAIS. Helped organize the 2023 "Statement on AI Risk" letter signed by 500+ AI leaders warning that "mitigating the risk of extinction from AI should be a global priority." Background in AI safety research and community-build

name: Lauren Mangla
notes: Chief Operating Officer of the AI Futures Project, the nonprofit that produced the AI 2027 scenario forecast. name: Philipp Hennig
notes: Chair for Methods of Machine Learning at University of Tübingen. Co-director of Tübingen AI Center since July 2025. Pioneer of probabilistic numerics — treating numerical computation as machine learning inference. Author of 'Probabilistic Numerics: Computation as Machine Learning' (Cambridge University Press, 2022). Previously at Max Planck Institute for Intelligent Systems. Organizer of ProbNum c

name: Lily Sands
notes: Chief of Staff at Secure AI Project. Background as Foreign Affairs Officer at U.S. Department of State and former Naval Officer (Anti-Submarine Warfare Officer, Electronic Warfare Officer). name: Eric Gastfriend

name: Meia Chita-Tegmark

name: Eric Ho

name: Kim Stanley Robinson

name: Nate Thomas

name: Charlie Rogers-Smith

name: Rudolf Laine

name: Esben Kran

name: Jonathan Claybrough

name: Tristan Cook

name: Anni Leskelä
notes: Conceptual researcher at the Center on Long-Term Risk (CLR). Works on multi-agent AI dynamics and cooperation theory. Published work on commitment and credibility in multipolar AI scenarios. Developed CLR's robustness framework distinguishing outcome robustness (net-positive direction) from implementation robustness (avoiding unintended negative effects). name: Prasanna (Sonny) Tambe
notes: Associate Professor of Operations, Information and Decisions at Wharton; Faculty Co-Director of Wharton Human-AI Research. Research focuses on economics of technology and labor: AI for HR applications, labor markets for AI skills, and how workers specialize in technologies. name: Herb Simon
notes: DECEASED (1916-2001). Nobel laureate (Economics, 1978) and AI pioneer at Carnegie Mellon University. Co-created Logic Theorist (1956) and General Problem Solver with Allen Newell and Cliff Shaw. Founded concept of bounded rationality. ACM Turing Award (1975, with Newell). At CMU from 1949 to 2001, instrumental in founding the School of Computer Science, GSIA, and multiple departments. CMU's Simon

name: Valerie Wirtschafter

name: Adrian Weller
notes: Director of Research in Machine Learning at the University of Cambridge; Programme Director for Trust and Society at the Leverhulme Centre for the Future of Intelligence (CFI). MBE. Member of UNESCO Ad Hoc Expert Group on Ethics

name: Aaron Courville
notes: Full Professor at Université de Montréal and founding core member of Mila. Co-authored the seminal Deep Learning textbook with Bengio and Goodfellow. Holds a CIFAR AI Chair. Incoming scientific director of IVADO as of 2025.

name: David Chalmers
notes: University Professor of Philosophy and Neural Science at NYU. Co-director of Center for Mind, Brain, and Consciousness. Formulated the "hard problem of consciousness." Leading voice on AI consciousness and moral status. Author of "Reality+" on virtual worlds. Warns against corporate control of virtual/AI worlds.

name: Paolo Bova
notes: Independent AI governance researcher and member of the Modeling Cooperation team. Economics graduate from University of Cambridge (2019). PhD student at Teesside University studying AI safety dynamics through behavioral and network modeling. name: Eoghan Stafford
notes: AAAS Science & Technology Policy Fellow. Former Research Scholar at the Centre for the Governance of AI (GovAI). PhD from Oxford/Future of Humanity Institute. name: Emmett Shear

Co-founder and CEO of Softmax, a 10-person AI startup focused on "organic alignment" — teaching AIs to cooperate with humans using principles from biology and collective intelligence. Backed by a16z. Previously co-founded Stem AI (incorporated June 2023).

name: Ken Stanley
notes: SVP of Open-Endedness at Lila Sciences. Previously led the Open-Endedness team at OpenAI (2020-2022) as Research Science Manager. Former professor of CS at University of Central Florida. name: Clark Barrett
notes: Professor (Research) of Computer Science at Stanford University. Director of Stanford Center for Automated Reasoning (Centaur) and co-director of Stanford Center for AI Safety. name: Bryan Parno
notes: Kavčić-Moura Professor of Electrical & Computer Engineering and Computer Science at Carnegie Mellon University. Senior Member of ACM and IEEE. Created the Verifiable Computation framework (reduced verification costs by 20+ orders of magnitude). Led Ironclad, Everest, and Verus projects for

name: Steve Omohundro
notes: AI safety pioneer. Founder and CEO of Beneficial AI Research (formerly Self-Aware Systems). Published the seminal 2008 paper "The Basic AI Drives," which argued that rational goal-seeking systems will develop drives for self-improvement and self-protection unless explicitly countered — a foundational concept in AI alignment theory. Advisor to the Machine Intelligence Research Institute (MIRI). Pro

name: David Duvenaud
notes: Associate Professor of Computer Science and Statistics at the University of Toronto. Canada Research Chair in Generative Models, Canada CIFAR AI Chair, Sloan Fellow, and founding member of the Vector Institute. Director at the AI Safety Foundation (AISF). name: Roger Grosse

name: Fynn Heide

name: Michael Genesereth

name: Jim Risch

name: Sven Herrmann

name: Daniel Clothiaux
notes: PhD student in the Language Technologies Department at Carnegie Mellon University. Research interests include philosophy of AI, philosophy of information, and computer simulation. name: Karl Berzins

name: Marc Warner

name: Linda Linsefors

Effective altruist focused on reducing existential risk from AI.

name: Ondřej Havlíček

name: Rob Fergus
notes: Rob Fergus is a computer scientist and Director of AI Research / Head of FAIR (Fundamental AI Research) at Meta. He co-founded FAIR with Yann LeCun in 2013 and co-founded the CILVR Lab at NYU in 2009. Fergus left Meta in 2020 to join Google DeepMind as a research director, then returned to Meta in May 2025 to lead FAIR, replacing Joelle Pineau. name: Nora Ammann
notes: Technical specialist at the Advanced Research and Invention Agency (ARIA) in the UK, serving as Programme Director for ARIA's Safeguarded AI programme. Co-founded and led Principles of Intelligence (PIBBSS), a research organization facilitating knowledge transfer from interdisciplinary sciences into AI safety. name: Jenny Marron
notes: Executive Director of the Institute for AI Policy and Strategy (IAPS), a nonpartisan think tank producing policy research on implications of AI from today's advanced models to potential AGI and superintelligence.
